member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Addon.EPacketDestination.Everyone
member.summary: NOTE: The host will send the sender's packet back to them. This is usually recommended.

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Addon.EPacketDestination.EveryoneExceptSender
member.summary: WARNING: Avoid using this if you don't know what you're doing. Incorrect use of it can cause desync.

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration
member.summary:
Derivatives are singletons. Get them with LITPacketRegistration.Get<T>(). Do not instantiate them.
To use, create your own class for your packet and inherit the base: MyCustomPacket : LITPacketRegistration.
Register it in your plugin's Awake() function with LITPacketRegistration.Get<MyCustomPacket>().Register();
Send it with LITPacketRegistration.Get<MyCustomPacket>().SendBool(), .SendString(), .SentStringAndBool(), or .SendByteArray()
Make sure that your derived class is in a defined namespace to avoid potential ambiguous GUID generations

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.Get``1
member.summary: Gets or creates the singleton instance of a packet registration.
member.typeparam: The type of your derived class. MyCustomPacket : LITPacketRegistration.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.OnPacketReceived(LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.Packet)
member.summary: Invoked when the packet is received. NOTE: This is NEVER called unless if Fika is installed.
member.param: Get needed data from packet with: packet.BoolData, packet.StringData, or packet.ByteArrayData

member.name: P:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.Destination
member.summary: Who the packet will be sent to.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.OnPacketSent(LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.Packet)
member.summary: Invoked on the sender client every time the packet is sent. NOTE: This is never called unless Fika is installed.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.Register
member.summary: Registers packet. Highly recommended to register packet in plugin's Awake() function.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITPacketRegistration.SendData(System.Object)
member.summary: Sends a packet containing data given. This function does nothing if Fika is not installed.

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITFikaTools
member.summary: Exposed external tools to be used primarily by Leave It There addons

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITFikaTools.IAmHost
member.summary: Returns true if Fika is not installed, or if it is and this client is the host of the raid.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITFikaTools.GetRaidId
member.summary: Returns profile id if Fika is not installed, or the id of the raid if it is (which will be the host's profile id).

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents
member.summary: All these events are static! Make sure to clean up subscriptions if needed!

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents.OnItemPlacedStateChanged
member.summary: Invoked every time an item is placed or reclaimed, including if it is re-placed in cases like being moved via move mode, or a packet received telling with instructions to place at X position, etc.

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents.OnFakeItemInitialized
member.summary: Invoked once every time the item is placed, when it was not already placed. Will not be invoked if item is moved.

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents.OnFakeItemReclaimed
member.summary: Invoked when an item is reclaimed. Will not be invoked if item is moved.

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents.OnPlacedItemSpawned
member.summary: Invoked after LeaveItThere spawns a placed item and creates / initializes its FakeItem component on raid start.

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents.OnRaidEnd
member.summary: Invoked just before LeaveItThere ModSession sends data to the server. This is the latest possible point where AddonData should be added.

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Addon.LITStaticEvents.OnLastPlacedItemSpawned
member.summary: Invoked after LeaveItThere spawns the last placed item, ideal time for checking if items exist or fetching their AddonData

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Common.CustomInteraction.#ctor
member.summary: If this constructor is used, FakeItem will not be set. Be sure not to use it unless you use the constructor overload that takes a FakeItem as an argument.

member.name: P:LeaveItThere.Common.CustomInteraction.TargetName
member.summary: Text that shows under the interaction prompt. Only works if it is on the first interaction in the list. GetActionsTypesClassList finds the first TargetName and applies it to the first interaction, but if you are using GetActionsTypesClass you'll have to manually ensure that the first interaction has the desired TargetName set.

member.name: P:LeaveItThere.Common.CustomInteraction.Enabled
member.summary: If returns false, interaction prompt will be greyed out and not selectable.

member.name: P:LeaveItThere.Common.CustomInteraction.AutoPromptRefresh
member.summary: If returns true, the interaction will auto refresh after Action() is called. This will update any names or states of the interaction, but will also reset the selected action to the first one.

member.name: P:LeaveItThere.Common.CustomInteraction.Name
member.summary: Name of interaction item in prompt.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Common.CustomInteraction.OnInteract
member.summary: Interaction callback. Will be called when an interaction is interacted with.

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Common.InteractionExamples.SuperSimpleExample
member.summary: Simple example that uses a FakeItem input

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Common.InteractionExamples.SuperSimpleExample.#ctor(LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem)
member.summary: Simple example that uses a FakeItem input

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Common.InteractionExamples.ExampleThatRunsCodeOnceWhenCreated
member.summary: This example is good for if you need to initialize another class instance once and store a reference to it in a property or field to be later accessed by the interaction callback

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Common.InteractionExamples.AllTheOtherThingsExample
member.summary: Shows more potential overrides (note that overriding is unnecessary if the default value is desired, such as Enabled defaulting to true). Also note that you don't HAVE to give the interaction a FakeItem. But, of course if you do not, then you won't be able to use the FakeItem property.

member.name: T:LeaveItThere.Common.InteractionExamples.HowToUse
member.summary: Ideally in OnFakeItemInitialized callback, add new instances of your interactions to the FakeItem's Actions list.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Components.ItemMover.UpdatePlayerLastFrameDeltas
member.summary: Should be called in OnEnable() and at the end of Update() to keep track of last frame's player pos

member.name: P:LeaveItThere.Components.ItemMover.PlayerMovementDelta
member.summary: Will only be correct while ItemMover is enabled

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.LITSession.CostOverrides
member.summary: Key = item template id, Value = the desired cost

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Components.LITSession.GetGlobalAddonDataOrNull``1(System.String)
member.summary: Fetches Global Addon Data by it's key, returns null if none is found. T must be a class.
member.typeparam: Must be a class. After Addon Data is fetched once, it can usually be modified directly without needing to use PutGlobalAddonData again since classes are stored by reference.
member.param: Key that Addon Data was saved under.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Components.LITSession.PutGlobalAddonData``1(System.String,``0)
member.summary: Saves Global Addon Data by a given key. Data will be saved to user/profiles/LeaveItThere-ItemData. Will be globally accessible when fetched, data is not associated with any specific item.
member.param: Key used to fetch the data later.

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.AddonFlags.MoveModeDisabled
member.summary: When true, the Move interaction is disabled.

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.AddonFlags.MoveModeDisabledReason
member.summary: Reason displayed for Move interaction being disabled in context menu

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.AddonFlags.ReclaimInteractionDisabled
member.summary: When true, the Reclaim interaction is disabled.

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.AddonFlags.ReclaimInteractionDisabledReason
member.summary: Reason displayed for Reclaim interaction being disabled in context menu

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.AddonFlags.IsPhysicalRegardlessOfSize
member.summary: Set to true to make this FakeItem ignore size restrictions and always be have player collision / block bot pathing.

member.name: F:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.AddonFlags.RemoveRootCollider
member.summary: When true, the root BoxCollider component that is added to LootItems will be removed. Useful for when a custom item has a child object defining a custom collider to be used for interactions.

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.OnSpawned
member.summary: Invoked if this FakeItem was just spawned by LITSession at start of raid

member.name: E:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.OnPlacedStateChanged
member.summary: Invoked any time the placed state of the FakeItem is changed. Unike OnFakeItemInitialized, it will be invoked when item is moved.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.GetAddonDataOrNull``1(System.String)
member.summary: Fetches Addon Data on this FakeItem by it's key, returns null if none is found. T must be a class.
member.typeparam: Must be a class. After Addon Data is fetched once, it can usually be modified directly without needing to use PutAddonData again since classes are stored by reference.
member.param: Key that Addon Data was saved under.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Components.FakeItem.PutAddonData``1(System.String,``0)
member.summary: Saves Addon Data by a given key. Data will be saved to user/profiles/LeaveItThere-ItemData under the FakeItem that the data is added to.
member.param: Key used to fetch the data later.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.CustomUI.ButtonDrag.Init(UnityEngine.RectTransform)
member.summary: Component should be added to the UI element that needs to be clicked in order to initiate dragging (like a button)

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Helpers.ItemHelper.SetupItem(EFT.InventoryLogic.Item,UnityEngine.Vector3,UnityEngine.Quaternion)
member.summary: Reimplementation of GameWorld.SetupItem that makes sure collisionDetectionMode is set correctly before rb is made to be kinematic to avoid unity warning spam.

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Helpers.LITUtils.ForAllDescendants(UnityEngine.GameObject,System.Action{UnityEngine.GameObject})
member.summary: 对 GameObject 的所有子物体（递归）执行操作，无 GC 分配。

member.name: M:LeaveItThere.Helpers.LITUtils.ServerRouteAsync``1(System.String,``0)
member.summary: Fire-and-forget 异步 HTTP POST，用于非阻塞保存数据。

member.name: T:ConfigurationManagerAttributes
member.summary:
Class that specifies how a setting should be displayed inside the ConfigurationManager settings window.
Usage: This class template has to be copied inside the plugin's project and referenced by its code directly. make a new instance, assign any fields that you want to override, and pass it as a tag for your setting.
If a field is null (default), it will be ignored and won't change how the setting is displayed. If a field is non-null (you assigned a value to it), it will override default behavior.
member.remarks: You can read more and see examples in the readme at https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx.ConfigurationManager You can optionally remove fields that you won't use from this class, it's the same as leaving them null.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.ShowRangeAsPercent
member.summary: Should the setting be shown as a percentage (only use with value range settings).

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.CustomDrawer
member.summary: Custom setting editor (OnGUI code that replaces the default editor provided by ConfigurationManager). See below for a deeper explanation. Using a custom drawer will cause many of the other fields to do nothing.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.Browsable
member.summary: Show this setting in the settings screen at all? If false, don't show.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.Category
member.summary: Category the setting is under. Null to be directly under the plugin.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.DefaultValue
member.summary: If set, a "Default" button will be shown next to the setting to allow resetting to default.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.HideDefaultButton
member.summary: Force the "Reset" button to not be displayed, even if a valid DefaultValue is available.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.HideSettingName
member.summary: Force the setting name to not be displayed. Should only be used with a to get more space. Can be used together with to gain even more space.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.Description
member.summary: Optional description shown when hovering over the setting. Not recommended, provide the description when creating the setting instead.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.DispName
member.summary: Name of the setting.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.Order
member.summary: Order of the setting on the settings list relative to other settings in a category. 0 by default, higher number is higher on the list.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.ReadOnly
member.summary: Only show the value, don't allow editing it.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.IsAdvanced
member.summary: If true, don't show the setting by default. User has to turn on showing advanced settings or search for it.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.ObjToStr
member.summary: Custom converter from setting type to string for the built-in editor textboxes.

member.name: F:ConfigurationManagerAttributes.StrToObj
member.summary: Custom converter from string to setting type for the built-in editor textboxes.
